steps.title: Your Journey Begins
steps.content: You're starting as a 14-year-old learning essential life skills. By age 18, you'll be ready for independence!

steps.title: Time System
steps.content: Time progresses automatically. Use the pause button and speed controls at the top of the screen. Each game minute = 1 real second (at 1x speed).

steps.title: Exploring the City
steps.content: Click on buildings in the 3D city to visit different locations. Use the camera controls to rotate and zoom.

steps.title: Building Your Future
steps.content: Complete chores, attend school, do homework, get a job, and save money. Every choice matters!

steps.title: Daily Chores
steps.content: Your parents assign 3-4 chores each day. Complete them to earn money and improve your skills!

steps.title: Learning by Doing
steps.content: Some chores have interactive tutorials that teach you the proper way to complete them.

steps.title: Building Habits
steps.content: Completing chores consistently teaches responsibility and time management - skills you'll need when you're independent.

steps.title: Attending Class
steps.content: School runs Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 1:20 PM. Don't be late! Tardies affect your record.

steps.title: Homework Matters
steps.content: Complete homework every day to improve your grades. Good grades = better college opportunities.

steps.title: GPA Calculation
steps.content: Your Grade Point Average (GPA) is calculated from all your class grades. Colleges use GPA for admissions.

steps.title: Extracurriculars
steps.content: Join clubs and activities! They improve your college applications and teach valuable skills.

steps.title: Cash vs. Bank
steps.content: Keep money in the bank for safety. Cash is convenient but can be lost or stolen.

steps.title: Earning Money
steps.content: Earn money from chores, part-time jobs, and eventually full-time work. Build your savings!

steps.title: Budgeting
steps.content: Track what you earn and spend. Make sure you have enough for bills, groceries, and savings.

steps.title: Emergency Fund
steps.content: Try to save at least $500 for unexpected expenses. This is called an emergency fund.

steps.title: What is a Credit Card?
steps.content: A credit card lets you borrow money from the bank. You must pay it back - with interest!

steps.title: Interest Rates
steps.content: If you don't pay your full balance each month, you're charged interest (usually 18-24% APR). This adds up fast!

steps.title: Credit Limit
steps.content: Your credit limit is the maximum you can borrow. Don't max it out - use less than 30% for good credit.

steps.title: Best Practice
steps.content: Only charge what you can afford to pay off immediately. Treat it like cash, not free money!

steps.title: Build Your Resume
steps.content: A resume shows employers your education, skills, and experience. Create one before applying to jobs.

steps.title: Job Requirements
steps.content: Different jobs have different requirements: age, GPA, or specific skills. Make sure you meet them!

steps.title: Being Punctual
steps.content: Always arrive on time! Three late arrivals = you're fired. Punctuality shows responsibility.

steps.title: Building Experience
steps.content: Even entry-level jobs teach valuable skills and look good on future applications.

steps.title: Welcome to Adulthood!
steps.content: At 18, you can rent an apartment, get a credit card, and vote. But with freedom comes responsibility!

steps.title: Monthly Bills
steps.content: If you move out, you'll pay rent, electricity, water, and more. Bills are due every month - late fees apply!

steps.title: Buying Groceries
steps.content: Parents no longer provide food. Budget about $50/week for groceries. Plan meals to save money!

steps.title: Financial Planning
steps.content: Make sure your income covers all expenses: rent + bills + food + savings. If not, get a better job or reduce expenses.

steps.title: Why College?
steps.content: College graduates earn more over their lifetime and have more career opportunities. It's an investment in your future.

steps.title: GPA Requirements
steps.content: Different colleges require different GPAs. Community colleges: 2.0+, State: 3.0+, Private: 3.5+, Ivy League: 3.8+

steps.title: Application Costs
steps.content: Applications cost $50-$100 each. Save money during high school so you can apply to multiple schools.

steps.title: Scholarships
steps.content: Scholarships are FREE MONEY for college! Apply to as many as possible. Good grades and extracurriculars help.

steps.title: Tuition Planning
steps.content: College is expensive! Save money, apply for scholarships, and consider starting at community college to save costs.
